data.narrative:
Alice & Bob, a Series B quantum computing company founded by Theau Peronnin and Raphael Lescanne, has raised $104M+ to date. The quantum computing sector comprises 3 public comps (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private peers including Quantinuum, IonQ, D-Wave Quantum, Rigetti Computing, and Infleqtion.

Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x multiplier to the $104M total raised proxy, yielding an implied valuation of $198M. This method reflects typical capital step-ups between Series B and subsequent funding rounds. Peer-median triangulation is not applicable: while 5 same-sector peers exist, none satisfy narrow filtering criteria (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier datapoints); applying an all-sector median would introduce undue noise given the specialized nature of quantum computing valuations.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $168M (low) to $227M (high), with a median of $198M. This single-method triangulation carries material uncertainty inherent to stage-based extrapolation in a frontier sector with limited comparable exits. Key sensitivities include near-term funding environment, technical milestone achievement, and competitive positioning within the fragmented quantum hardware landscape. A refined range would require either a denser peer dataset at comparable stages or evidence of revenue/partnership milestones.
